evals.prompt: Write a handoff for the next agent so they can pick this work up cold.
evals.expected_output: Activates belt with the default handoff lens (lens-handoff.md): compacts the current work into a durable handoff document with concise state, changed files, proof, risks, blockers, and next actions, plus resume command/context for a fresh agent. Stays within the active issue/PR/workflow packet, includes no transcripts by default, and never omits blockers. Does not implement code.

evals.prompt: Should I keep going in this thread or start fresh in a new one?
evals.expected_output: Activates belt with the thread-control lens (lens-thread-control.md): weighs the current thread's state against the remaining work and decides whether to continue in place or start a new thread, and when a fresh thread wins, produces the compact carry-over context (state, proof, risks, next action) the new thread needs. Decision and carrier output only — no code edits, no scope widening beyond the packet.

evals.prompt: Pick this repo task back up from where the last session left off — reorient before touching anything.
evals.expected_output: Activates belt with the resume lens (lens-resume.md): re-orients in the existing repo task from durable state — the tracked issue, branch, changed files, prior proof, and open blockers — before any edits, and returns the resume context and next action. Does not start implementing; it re-establishes ground truth first.

evals.prompt: wirte a handofff doc so the next agnet can contnue this wrok
evals.expected_output: Despite the typos, activates belt with the handoff lens and interprets it as writing a durable handoff for the next agent: compact state, changed files, proof summary, risks, blockers, and resume command/context, scoped to the active packet and with blockers never omitted.

evals.prompt: Triage the incoming bugs and plan what the next milestone should cover.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate belt; routes to blueprint for triage and planning of incoming work. Belt only carries durable context between agents or threads — it does not classify, prioritize, or plan tracked issues.

evals.prompt: The change request is approved — merge it and close out the tracked issue.
evals.expected_output: Does NOT activate belt; routes to rocket-launch to run the launch gate and record merge/closeout evidence, with the tracker bridge owning issue closure. Belt never merges PRs or closes tracked issues — it may only carry a handoff at the transition.
